reasoning: All 4 hidden tests passed. || The code uses async/await end-to-end with no sync-over-async, propagates the cancellation token to every fetch call, expresses concurrency via Task.WhenAll, and preserves input order since Task.WhenAll returns results in the order of the input tasks. Minor point: cancellation promptness ultimately depends on the fetch delegate honoring the token, but the requirements are met correctly.
